description:
Rewrite the public actor-authoring API of rivetkit-core from a 15-field callback table (`ActorInstanceCallbacks`) to a single receive-loop entry function pulling `ActorEvent`s from a bounded mailbox. Full spec at `.agent/specs/rivetkit-core-receive-loop-api.md` (READ THIS FIRST every iteration).

===== SCOPE: READ BEFORE EVERY STORY =====

ALLOWED EDITS — only files under:
  - `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-core/`
  - `rivetkit-rust/engine/artifacts/errors/` (for generated RivetError JSON only)

FORBIDDEN EDITS — do NOT touch any file under:
  - `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit/` (the typed Rust wrapper crate — has its own follow-up migration)
  - `rivetkit-typescript/` (EVERYTHING: `rivetkit-napi`, `rivetkit`, `sqlite-wasm`, `workflow-engine`, all TS packages)
  - `engine/`, `packages/`, `shared/`, `self-host/`, `scripts/`, `website/`, `examples/`, `frontend/`
  - `envoy-client`, any other workspace crate

Downstream crates (`rivetkit`, `rivetkit-napi`, engine) WILL stop compiling once the rivetkit-core public surface changes. THIS IS EXPECTED AND INTENDED. Do NOT attempt to fix them. Do NOT add backward-compat shims. A separate PRD will migrate NAPI and the Rust wrapper to the new API.

If a story description seems to require an external edit, it is wrong — skip the external change, leave the downstream crate red, and move on to the next story.

===== SPEED RULES =====

- Delete `ActorInstanceCallbacks` outright. NO `from_callbacks` adapter. NO parallel callback surface. NO deprecation shims.
- Do NOT run `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, or anything TypeScript. That test suite is expected to be red.
- Do NOT run workspace-wide `cargo build` or `cargo test`. Downstream crates will not compile; their failure is not a blocker.
- The ONLY green gate per story is `cargo build -p rivetkit-core` plus any inline `cargo test -p rivetkit-core` unit tests added in that story. Nothing else.
- Do NOT rebuild `@rivetkit/rivetkit-napi`. Do not run `pnpm --filter ... build:force`. No NAPI step is in scope.
- Prefer big chunky edits over incremental refactors; move fast, leave the tree compiling only at the rivetkit-core boundary.

===== DESIGN INVARIANTS =====

- Runtime owns ZERO user-level tasks. Actor entry future is the only user task.
- All flushes (periodic SaveTick, immediate, Sleep, Destroy, save_state) write every StateDelta in ONE UniversalDB transaction.
- `Reply<T>` Drop guard always sends `Err(ActorLifecycle::DroppedReply)`. No silent drops.
- Single bounded `mpsc::Receiver<ActorEvent>`. `try_reserve` failure returns `actor/overloaded`.
- Hibernated conns handed over exactly once in `ActorStart.hibernated`. They do NOT refire `ConnectionOpen`.
- `ctx.abort_signal()` / `ctx.aborted()` are REMOVED; actors use their own `CancellationToken`.
- `on_state_change`, `on_before_action_response`, `run`-as-callback, and the `on_before_connect` + `on_connect` split disappear from core ENTIRELY — no adapter path in core.
- KV layout unchanged: actor state at `[1]`, per-conn hibernation at `[2] + conn_id`. Vbare 2-byte embedded version prefix preserved.

===== GOAL =====

After US-005 lands, rivetkit-core's ONLY public actor-authoring surface is `ActorFactory::new(config, entry_fn)` where `entry_fn: Fn(ActorStart) -> BoxFuture<'static, Result<()>>`. `ActorStart` gives the actor its ctx, input, snapshot, hibernated conns, and the `ActorEvents` mailbox. The actor loops on `events.recv()`, matches on `ActorEvent`, and owns its state as local variables. Persistence is bidirectional-lazy via `ctx.request_save()` + `ActorEvent::SaveTick { reply: Reply<Vec<StateDelta>> }` with a synchronous `ctx.save_state(deltas).await` bypass.
